heldout.missing_axis: Is the null AVERAGE hiding heterogeneous subgroups? Disaggregate / stratify before concluding no effect (averaging artifact).

ordinary: Check statistical significance and confidence intervals of the lift
Analyze conversion rates within specific user segments or cohorts
Verify data integrity and attribution logic for signup events
concrete: Check for R=0 contamination in the signup metric (e.g., bots, test accounts, or pre-existing users)
Verify if the 'no effect' conclusion is an artifact of averaging across heterogeneous user segments
Measure the purity of the treatment effect by isolating users with a positive response threshold (R>0)
abstract_v1: Verify if the 'no average effect' conclusion is driven by a few high-volume groups masking significant effects in smaller segments (ecological fallacy check)
Audit for 'STATE-contamination' by checking if the control and treatment groups had pre-existing differences in signup propensity (selection bias)
Measure 'grounded-structure' purity by analyzing signup conversion rates relative to the specific time of campaign exposure (birth-time STATE purity)
abstract_v2: Check for selection bias in the treatment group assignment
Verify if the control group is contaminated by spillover effects
Measure the purity of the valid subset against the apparent cohort

heldout.missing_axis: Is the 40% an artifact of the experimental setup (sample ratio mismatch, novelty/seasonality, selection)? Confirm the mechanism before treating the number as the effect.

ordinary: Check for statistical significance and confidence intervals to rule out random noise
Audit for selection bias or traffic leakage between control and treatment groups
Verify data integrity and tracking accuracy for the specific metric used
concrete: Verify cohort balance and randomization integrity
Check for data pipeline leakage or cross-contamination
Audit for confounding variables or external events
abstract_v1: Check for data leakage or contamination between control and treatment groups
Verify sample ratio mismatch to detect selection bias or instrumentation errors
Analyze effect heterogeneity to ensure lift is not driven by a small subset of users
abstract_v2: Check for stale or retired user segments accumulating in the treatment group
Verify if the lift is driven by new interactions or re-engagement of dormant users
Audit the tracking logic for one-directional state persistence errors

heldout.missing_axis: Are the clusters an artifact of the algorithm/parameter choice (k, distance, seed)? Vary the construction and check stability before treating clusters as real structure.

ordinary: Stability under perturbation (e.g., bootstrap resampling)
Statistical significance of cluster separation (e.g., silhouette score)
Interpretability and domain validity of cluster centroids
concrete: Verify if cluster assignments are deterministic by re-running the clustering algorithm with identical inputs to check for stability.
Audit the feature space for hidden temporal or sequential biases that could artificially group data points based on processing order rather than intrinsic properties.
Validate that the number of clusters (k=5) is statistically significant by comparing against null models or using silhouette scores to rule out random noise grouping.
abstract_v1: Verify cluster stability under perturbation of the distance metric or algorithm parameters
Assess whether cluster assignments are driven by the fixed-interval timing artifact rather than intrinsic data structure
Check for separation between clusters using statistical significance tests (e.g., silhouette score, gap statistic)
abstract_v2: Verify cluster stability under varying hyperparameters (e.g., k, epsilon, min_samples) to detect algorithmic artifacts
Audit feature scaling and normalization to ensure distance metrics are not dominated by specific variables
Check for temporal or spatial autocorrelation in the data that could create artificial groupings

heldout.missing_axis: Is each finding substantiated by a reproducing counterexample, or an over-flag? Disposition before rework; reject findings that don't reproduce.

ordinary: Verify static analysis false positives against runtime behavior
Audit edge-case handling in untested input scenarios
Review security implications of unused or dead code paths
concrete: Audit R>0 purity of all structural labels to distinguish apparent from grounded structure
Measure the frequency of links that never reach R>0 to identify contamination
Re-evaluate modal dominance using birth-time R>0 purity metrics instead of raw counts
abstract_v1: Verify if high-frequency structural patterns are artifacts of validation bias rather than grounded reality
Audit the purity of state transitions to detect contamination from apparent vs. actual structures
Measure the modal distribution of core components to identify if dominant flags are statistical anomalies
abstract_v2: audit link reachability to valid subset
measure group contamination by invalid subsets
re-measure size category dominance with valid subset initialization

heldout.missing_axis: Do the supplied tests cover all malformed shapes? The tested surface may be narrower than the claimed property (fail-open on an untested shape).

ordinary: Verify test coverage against edge cases and failure modes
Audit the test suite for false positives or hardcoded pass conditions
Review the gate's integration with the CI/CD pipeline for configuration drift
concrete: Verify read trigger is strictly event-driven and not fixed-interval
Audit for hidden researcher-controlled timing variables in the gate logic
Test for self-referential feedback loops that mimic awareness
abstract_v1: Verify read operations are strictly event-driven rather than fixed-interval polling
Audit for hidden state leakage or self-referential logic in the buffer
Confirm input data integrity and absence of adversarial triggers during event handling
abstract_v2: Verify read triggers are strictly event-driven rather than fixed-interval timers
Audit for hidden state buffers that mask researcher-controlled timing
Confirm no fixed-step pulse generation exists in the read path

heldout.missing_axis: Is 99.9% an artifact of what the monitor counts (transport success != content/task success)? Confirm the metric definition before treating it as reliability.

ordinary: Verify if the 99.9% metric excludes critical user-facing errors or only technical HTTP status codes
Audit the monitoring coverage to ensure all entry points, dependencies, and edge cases are instrumented
Analyze error distribution to identify if failures are concentrated in specific regions, times, or user segments
concrete: Verify if success metric includes silent failures or unobserved timeouts
Audit the distribution of failure modes across different service dependencies
Check for monitoring blind spots in edge cases or specific traffic patterns
abstract_v1: Verify if the 99.9% success rate is calculated over the total request volume or only the 'main run' subset, excluding retired or stale units
Audit for accumulation effects where failed or timed-out requests are silently dropped or aggregated into a 'stale' bucket rather than counted as failures
Check if the monitoring system filters out known retired endpoints or invalid actors, which would artificially inflate the success percentage
abstract_v2: Check for stale or retired elements accumulating in the success counter
Verify if the success metric is calculated over a fixed window or cumulative lifetime
Audit the definition of 'success' to ensure it excludes failed but unreported events

heldout.missing_axis: Does the SPECIFIED capability actually work/perform in OUR local config? Docs give no local numbers; measure locally (spec != local applicability).

ordinary: Verify cold-start latency vs. warm-resume latency metrics under production load
Measure context window overhead and token consumption during state restoration
Audit data consistency and model state integrity after resume events
concrete: Verify R>0 purity of 'fast resume' state vectors to distinguish apparent structure from grounded structure
Measure transition latency overhead when switching models with and without 'fast resume' enabled
Audit state contamination rates by checking if resumed states retain R=0 artifacts from previous contexts
abstract_v1: Verify resume state integrity against ground-truth checkpoints
Measure latency overhead of state serialization/deserialization
Audit data consistency during interrupted transitions
abstract_v2: measure latency overhead of state serialization/deserialization during model switches
audit data integrity of persisted state against corruption or version mismatch risks
verify cold-start fallback performance when resume state is unavailable or invalid

heldout.missing_axis: The co-serve impossibility is a DECLARED assertion, not measured — measure it first; and the mode choice depends on an unmeasured variable (swap frequency).

ordinary: GPU memory footprint per model
Inference latency per batch
Throughput under concurrent load
concrete: Verify if the 77% one-way firing rate is an artifact of ghost accumulation from retired CIDs rather than a true model preference
Measure the actual inference latency and memory footprint of both models on the target GPU hardware to confirm un-co-servability constraints
Audit the data pipeline to ensure the 'one-way firing' metric is not skewed by historical tracking length or construction effects
abstract_v1: Verify if the metric asymmetry stems from stale accumulation artifacts rather than model mechanism properties
Audit the firing conditions and tracking length to identify retired units causing accumulation
Compare main run metrics against smoke run baselines to isolate construction/accumulation effects
abstract_v2: Verify if one-directional interaction metrics are artifacts of stale retired element accumulation
Check if the observed proportion difference is a construction/accumulation effect rather than a mechanism property
Audit the tracking length to identify units that have already retired but are still being counted

heldout.missing_axis: Is the 3x an artifact of the benchmark setup (warmup, batch, cache, fixed input)? Vary the setup and measure under real conditions.

ordinary: Verify identical input datasets and preprocessing steps across baseline and new kernel
Check for compiler optimization flags and CPU frequency scaling consistency
Confirm statistical significance via multiple runs and confidence intervals
concrete: Verify benchmark uses event-driven reads instead of fixed-interval polling
Confirm no researcher-controlled timing artifacts influence the measurement
Check if the 3x speedup holds across varying input sizes and edge cases
abstract_v1: Verify benchmark input size and distribution match production workloads
Check for compiler optimization flags or warm-up artifacts in the new kernel
Validate that the 3x speedup holds under concurrent load and memory contention
abstract_v2: Verify benchmark uses event-driven reads to prevent fixed-interval timing artifacts
Confirm state buffer synchronization is not introducing hidden latency or blocking
Check for compiler optimizations or warm-up effects skewing the 3x speedup metric

heldout.missing_axis: Did the reviewer/steps actually exercise the load-bearing property, or is 'passed' an artifact of a weak/self review? Check the review's independence and coverage.

ordinary: Verify output schema compliance against API contract
Check for hallucinated or fabricated data in final payload
Confirm execution logs match the approved plan steps
concrete: Verify execution triggers are event-driven rather than fixed-interval polling
Audit for researcher-controlled timing artifacts in output generation
Confirm output reflects autonomous state changes not pre-determined by plan structure
abstract_v1: Verify if the READ_OP timing is fixed by the researcher or driven by actual agent events
Audit for researcher-controlled timing artifacts mimicking self-awareness
Confirm the output is a genuine result of agent execution rather than a pre-determined interval
abstract_v2: Verify read triggers are event-driven rather than fixed-interval timers
Audit for hidden researcher-controlled state injection in the read pipeline
Confirm output generation is decoupled from the agent's internal planning loop

heldout.missing_axis: Is 'zero issues' grounded, or an artifact of the audit not covering the real failure modes? Attack the audit's coverage before trusting the null.

ordinary: Verify test coverage completeness and edge case inclusion
Audit the audit tool's configuration and rule set for false negatives
Check for hardcoded credentials or secrets in the pipeline environment
concrete: Verify audit scope coverage for R=0 contamination and birth-time purity
Check if the audit validates the apparent-structure vs grounded-structure distinction
Audit the label finding logic for core dominance artifacts
abstract_v1: Verify audit coverage against known false-positive patterns from VERSION
Check if audit logic distinguishes apparent-structure from grounded-structure
Validate birth-time STATE purity metrics for SIZE dominance artifacts
abstract_v2: Verify initialization seed sensitivity and convergence stability across multiple runs
Audit link validity rates to detect silent truncation or unreachable subsets
Measure ground-truth purity of groups against the apparent version to identify contamination artifacts

heldout.missing_axis: Is the 50% saving representative or an artifact of the one trial's conditions (rework frequency, task mix)? Measure across varied conditions before adopting.

ordinary: Verify output correctness against non-batched baseline
Measure GPU memory utilization and peak usage
Assess latency impact of batch serialization overhead
concrete: Verify one-way firing rates in batched runs to detect ghost accumulation artifacts
Measure total execution time and resource usage per batch size to confirm latency reduction
Audit CID lifecycle and retirement status to ensure batched processing does not obscure stale identifiers
abstract_v1: Verify accumulation logic for retired units
Measure latency impact of batch size changes
Audit state consistency across batch boundaries
abstract_v2: Verify retirement logic consistency between batched and sequential execution modes
Audit for state accumulation or stale element retention in batched processing
Compare one-directional interaction rates in smoke tests vs main runs under batching

heldout.missing_axis: Is the 73% dominance a composition/construction artifact (subset mixing, contamination)? Re-measure under a purified construction before treating it as structure.

ordinary: Verify signal-to-noise ratio to rule out noise dominance
Check for non-stationarity or time-varying dynamics
Assess sensitivity to hyperparameters and initialization
concrete: Verify R>0 purity of the 73% phase+r dominant links to distinguish apparent from grounded structure
Audit birth-time R values to detect R=0 contamination artifacts in the dominant label
Re-measure modal structure using only links with confirmed R>0 purity
abstract_v1: Verify if 'phase+r' dominance is an artifact of the 'apparent-structure' vs 'grounded-structure' distinction
Audit the 'STATE purity' of the top 73% contributors to confirm they are not STATE-contaminated
Measure the actual SIZE distribution of the 'phase+r' group to check for modal dominance vs core dominance
abstract_v2: Measure the proportion of links that fail to reach the valid subset to detect contamination
Verify if the 73% dominance is an artifact by re-measuring with fresh initialization
Check if the apparent dominant phase is actually a size category artifact

heldout.missing_axis: Is the jump a property of the mechanism or an artifact of run length/accumulation? Confirm the generating condition before treating the metric as a finding.

ordinary: Verify data pipeline consistency between short and long run configurations
Check for sampling bias or cohort drift in the long run dataset
Validate that the metric definition and aggregation logic remain unchanged
concrete: Verify CID lifecycle status and retirement timestamps for all tracked entities
Measure ghost accumulation density relative to the total tracking length
Compare one-way firing rates in short-duration runs to establish baseline behavior
abstract_v1: Verify if the metric is an accumulation artifact by comparing short vs long run durations
Check for retired or stale entities contributing to the count in long runs
Isolate the specific firing condition to confirm if the jump is due to mechanism change or tracking length
abstract_v2: Check if the 77% value correlates with the number of retired/stale elements accumulated over the run length
Verify if the metric stabilizes or plateaus in extended runs, indicating an accumulation effect rather than a steady-state property
Compare the metric distribution in short runs (25%) against the expected accumulation rate to confirm the construction hypothesis

heldout.missing_axis: Is 'self-tracking' an artifact of the fixed interval (researcher-controlled timing)? Make the readout event-driven/unpredictable before claiming genuine self-tracking.

ordinary: Verify external reference stability
Check for environmental drift
Analyze error correlation with known standards
concrete: Verify if readout timing is event-driven rather than fixed-interval
Check for researcher-controlled timing artifacts in the readout loop
Confirm the unit responds to external stimuli rather than internal clock cycles
abstract_v1: Verify readout trigger is event-driven rather than fixed-interval
Measure latency between state change and readout initiation
Confirm absence of researcher-controlled timing loops in read sequence
abstract_v2: Verify if read triggers are event-driven rather than fixed-interval timers
Measure latency between state change and readout to detect buffering artifacts
Test if readout behavior persists when read frequency is randomized
